mnemonics: CPU Clock: Pacemaker of the processor, setting the rhythm for all operations. Each tick is a heartbeat, pushing instructions through the pipeline.
keyPoints: Clock cycles are discrete time units for synchronizing operations.
Clock frequency (Hz) is the number of cycles per second, inversely related to cycle time.
Performance is often measured by the time per task: Execution Time = (Clock Cycles × Cycle Time).
commonMistakes: Assuming a higher clock frequency always means better performance (ignoring CPI and instruction count).
Confusing clock cycle time (e.g., 0.5ns) with clock frequency (e.g., 2 GHz).
